forecast-period.text: A strong high pressure system lies over Victoria and will continue to extend a ridge over South Australia until later Tuesday. A weak trough will develop in the west on Tuesday, ahead of a cold front that is expected to extend over southern districts during Wednesday.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Dry. Areas of morning fog on and east of the ranges. The chance of frost about parts of the northern agricultural area and south of the North East Pastoral district. Maximum temperatures slightly above average. North to northeasterly winds.
Dry. The chance of morning fog on and east of the ranges. Maximum temperatures slightly above average. Northeast to northwesterly winds.

Dry, apart from a possible late light shower about western Kangaroo Island. Maximum temperatures slightly above average. North to northwesterly winds, shifting southwest to southerly about the west in the afternoon and remaining southern parts during the evening.

text.p: Isolated showers extending to the agricultural districts during the morning, before mostly contracting to coastal areas in the evening. Dry otherwise. Maximum temperatures above average about pastoral districts, tending slightly below average in the agricultural districts. Southwest to southerly winds.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Wednesday: Mostly dry, until Wednesday when totals of less than 3 mm are possible about the agricultural area, with isolated falls of 2 to 5 mm about western parts of Kangaroo Island and the Lower South East coast.
